post.summary: Log into Pentera API and generate a token
post.description:
Use the API Client ID and TGT generated in Pentera's user interface (web app) to authenticate.
The call returns a TGT and a token. The TGT is valid for 180 days, the token is cycled with every call.
Every API call returns a fresh token in its response metadata, for use in the subsequent call.
The TGT is used whenever authentication is required.

parameters.name: Login Params
parameters.description: Submit an API Client ID and TGT. To generate a API Client ID and initial TGT, open your Pentera web application and navigate to Administration > Integrations > API Clients.

tgt.description: Long-term TGT for authentication that is valid for 180 days. To issue an initial TGT, open your Pentera web application and navigate to Administration > Integrations > API Clients.

token.description:
Short-term token for running a single Pentera API call. A fresh token is supplied in the response metadata of every endpoint, ready for use to make a subsequent call.
The token's lifetime is configurable from the Pentera UI under Administration > MISC. Settings > Automatically Log Out Idle Users.
By default, the token expires after 30 minutes.

get.summary: Fetch testing history for a specified time frame
get.description: Fetches metadata for all test runs that were executed between the start and end dates.

parameters.name: start_timestamp
parameters.description: Earliest test run to include in the data, based on the date that the test run was initiated. (Milliseconds since Unix Epoch, integer format recommended)

parameters.name: end_timestamp
parameters.description: Latest test run to include in the data, based on the date that the test run ended. (Milliseconds since Unix Epoch, integer format recommended)

200.description: List of test runs run during the specified timeframe and their metadata

403.description: The current user does not have the necessary privileges to make this request

get.summary: Fetch achievements for a single test run
get.description: Fetches all of Pentera's achievements for a specific test run

parameters.name: task_run_id
parameters.description: test run ID

400.description: Test run ID is not formatted correctly

403.description: The current user does not have the necessary privileges to make this request

404.description: Requested test run ID does not exist

parameters.description: Testing Scenario template used to configure the task

post.description: Stops a test run. Once stopped, the test enters the cleanup phase before it ends. Once cleanup is complete, the next test can be initiated.

parameters.name: task_run_id
parameters.description: ID of the test to be stopped.

200.description: Test run was successfully stopped. Please allow for the test cleanup to complete before initiating the next test run.

400.description: Test run ID is not formatted correctly

403.description: The current user does not have the necessary privileges for this request

404.description: Requested test run ID is not currently running or does not exist

get.description: Fetches details for a specific asset within the scope of a specific test run

parameters.name: task_run_id
parameters.description: Test run ID

parameters.name: target_type
parameters.description: Target categorization, for example, host, server, router, etc. It is returned by the endpoint `/task_run/{task_run_id}/achievements`

parameters.name: target_id
parameters.description: The Target ID is specific to the test run. It is returned by the endpoint `/task_run/{task_run_id}/achievements`

200.description: Success! Asset details for the specific test are provided below

400.description: Test run ID is not formatted correctly

403.description: The current user does not have the necessary privileges for this request

404.description: Requested test run ID does not exist

405.description: The requested Target Type is not supported by this endpoint.

post.summary: Clone and edit a Testing Scenario template
post.description:
Create a new template on the basis of an existing template with a new name, description, and IP ranges to be covered.
The schedule is automatically reset. The Attack Interface and other settings are carried over from the original template and can be edited from the Pentera UI.

parameters.name: template_id
parameters.description: ID of the Testing Scenario template to be cloned

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name for the new Testing Scenario template

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Description for the new Testing Scenario template

parameters.name: IP ranges
parameters.description: Define the IP ranges to be covered by the new Testing Scenario template

id.description: Unique ID of the new Testing Scenario template

name.description: Name of the new Testing Scenario template

description.description: Description of the new Testing Scenario template

schedule.description: Testing schedule copied from the original template. This schedule needs to be changed from the Pentera UI

ip_ranges.description: IP ranges to be covered by the Testing Scenario template

exclude_ip_ranges.description: IP ranges to be excluded by the Testing Scenario template

400.description: Test ID is not formatted correctly

403.description: The current user does not have the necessary privileges to make this request

404.description: Requested Test ID does not exist

get.summary: Recalculate test run stats in case something went wrong
get.description: In cases all vulnerabilities and achievements count in the overview and the report are zeros or some differance between the results exists in cases something went wrong during the run, this API can recalculate to make sure the numbers are now correct

parameters.name: task_run_id
parameters.description: test run ID

200.description: Test run stats was successfully recalculated. Please check the test overview to see if the number are now correct.

400.description: Test run ID is not formatted correctly

403.description: The current user does not have the necessary privileges to make this request

404.description: Requested test run ID does not exist

post.summary: Delete multiple test runs in bulk
post.description: Deletes a list of test runs specified by their IDs.

get.description: Fetch a status for all deployed nodes, including the node’s name, connection status, IP address, and version.

403.description: The current user does not have the necessary privileges to make this request

get.description: Returns the prefix and postfix that were in effect when the specified task run executed. These reflect the identifiers at execution time and may differ from the system’s current values. Prefix and/or postfix identifiers are used in Pentera’s Silent Run feature for reducing noise in monitoring systems.

parameters.name: task_run_id
parameters.description: ID of the test run

scheduleType.description: The current system scheduleType for command-line identifier rotation (e.g. "daily" or "off"). This reflects the config in effect now, not the schedule that was active during the task run.

lastGenerationTime.description: The timestamp when the system last generated new command-line identifiers . This comes from the current config and is not tied to the specific task run.

400.description: Test ID is not formatted correctly

403.description: The current user does not have the necessary privileges to make this request

404.description: Requested Test ID does not exist

get.description: Returns the current list of IPs and hostnames excluded from proxy routing.

post.description: Overwrites the list of IPs and hostnames that should bypass the proxy.

message.example: Invalid entries: '@bad-name. Only valid IP / DNS hostnames are allowed.
Maximum number of exclusions exceeded. Limit is 100 entries.

client_id.description: An API Client ID can be issued in your Pentera UI. Open your Pentera Platform and navigate to Administration > Integrations > API Clients to create or copy an existing Client ID.

tgt.description: A TGT can be issued in your Pentera UI. Open your Pentera Platform and navigate to Administration > Integrations > API Clients to copy or  issue a new TGT for the Client ID.

id.description: Internal Pentera ID per achievement type

creation_time.description: Time and date as it appears in the Pentera Attack Map, indicating when Pentera obtained the achievement. (Epoch datetime in milliseconds.)

summary.description: Synopsis of the Pentera's ethical hacking achievement

parameters.description: Input parameters used by Pentera to execute the action and obtain the achievement.

insight.description: Synopsis of the attack vector that led to the achievement and/or the expected adversary next steps.
insight.example: An attacker may list and read sensitive source code data which might provide find new vulnerabilities and exploit them

task_run_id.description: Test run ID. Every time a Testing Scenario template is run, it receives a unique ID.

start_timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the test run was initiated. (Milliseconds since Unix Epoch)

end_timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the test run ended and after cleanup was completed. (Milliseconds since Unix Epoch)

pentera_version.description: The version of Pentera Core used in the test

score.description:
The score received for the test represents the overall cyber posture of the tested environment on a scale from A to C or as a percentage.

Notes:
N/A means the testing duration was too short or there were insufficient findings
null means this testing scenario type does not support score

scorecard.description: NULL means this testing scenario type does not support scorecard

critical_assets.description: The critical assets score indicates if Critical Assets were compromised

credentials_account_takeover.description: The credentials and accounts takeover score indicates the success rate of account takeovers

sniffing.description: The sniffing score indicates the severity of sniffing findings

password_strength.description: The password strength score is based on the success rate of cracking actions

lateral_movement.description: The lateral movement score indicates the coverage of lateral movement across the network

accessible_data.description: The accessible data score indicate the level of access to data

host_takeover.description: The host takeover score indicates the success rate of host takeovers

av_edr_bypass.description: The AV/EDR score indicates the success rate of the remote command session attempts

id.description: Internal Pentera ID per vulnerability type

creation_time.description: Time as it appears in the Pentera Attack Map, indicating when Pentera validated that the vulnerability exists in the tested network

name.example: Discovered closed ports on the host

summary.example: Discovered closed port on the host (reachable without firewalling).

found_on.description: Array of IPs affected by the vulnerability in the tested network

priority.description: Remediation priority as determined by Pentera, based on the number of hosts affected by the vulnerability, the number and severity of achievements caused by it, and whether the vulnerability provided access to the network's Critical Assets, as configured in Pentera.

insight.description: Synopsis of the attack vectors enabled by the vulnerability
insight.example: Discovered closed port on the host (reachable without firewalling).

remediation.example: If closed ports are reachable through the firewall, they can be abused. It is recommended to block closed ports via firewalling to prevent malicious software from establishing a C2 channel through a closed port.

start_time.description: Time and date that the action was started (Epoch datetime in milliseconds.)

status.description: If successful, the action furthered Pentera's ethical attack. If `no results`, the action did not further the attack.

parameters.description: Input parameters used by Pentera to execute the action.

operation_type.example: Host Discovery with ICMP Timestamp Request

agent_name.description: Pentera node that executed the action

template_id.description: Unique ID of the Testing Scenario template

description.description: Short description of the Testing Scenario template

schedule.description: Testing schedule for the Testing Scenario template

last_start_time.description: Timestamp of when the template was most recently run (Unix timestamp in seconds), Might return '-1' in case of no test run for this template

next_scheduled_run.description: Next scheduled run for the Testing Scenario template

timezone.description: Time zone configured for the template. The time zone is configured per template in the Pentera UI

template_id.description: Unique ID of the Testing Scenario template

task_run_id.description: Test run ID. Each time a Testing Scenario template is run, it receives a new test run ID

start_time.description: Timestamp of when the template was most recently run (Unix timestamp in seconds)

enum: Targeted Testing - Windows Critical Vulnerability Scanning
Targeted Testing - AD Password Strength Assessment

id.description: The Host ID is specific to the test run

target.description: Pentera categorizes its targets by type. For this endpoint, the target is `host`

os_name.description: Operation System information for the host

os_type.description: OS type for the host, as in Windows, Linux, etc.

state.description: Port status, can be open, closed, filtered, etc.

transport.description: Transport protocol used by the service
